title: War in the Gulf Just Broke the Market—And the Fed Isn't Cutting Rates
description: Iran's attack on Qatar's largest LNG facility sent [Brent crude above $119 per barrel](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/19/oil-jumps-iran-strikes-qatar-lng-facility-supply-worries.html), wiping out 17% of Qatar's gas output. The [S&P 500 dropped 0.8%, Nasdaq fell 1%](https://tradingeconomics.com/united-states/stock-market), and all three major indices hit [four-month lows](https://tradingeconomics.com/united-states/stock-market). Meanwhile, [the Federal Reserve raised its inflation forecast to 2.7%](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/federal-reserve-fomc-march-18-interest-rate-decision/) and now seven FOMC members project zero rate cuts this year—a sharp reversal.
verdict: [Brent crude briefly climbed above $119 per barrel](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/19/oil-jumps-iran-strikes-qatar-lng-facility-supply-worries.html) after Iran's strike on Qatar's largest LNG facility, [wiping out 17% of the country's gas output](https://www.thestreet.com/investing/stock-market-today-mar-18-2026-stocks-futures-plumment-ahead-of-market-open-as-oil-headwinds-continue). The [S&P 500 fell 0.8%, hitting four-month lows](https://tradingeconomics.com/united-states/stock-market), while [the Fed raised its inflation forecast to 2.7% and signaled seven FOMC members now project zero rate cuts this year](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/18/fed-interest-rate-decision-march-2026.html). If you're in broad index funds or growth stocks, the data shows rising volatility, higher bond yields, and a geopolitical tail risk that wasn't priced in. [Bank of America says recession is unlikely](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/17/stock-market-today-live-updates.html)—but higher rates for longer is almost certain. You decide what that means for your portfolio.

content.headline: War in the Gulf Just Broke the Market—And the Fed Isn't Cutting Rates
content.summary: Iran's attack on Qatar's largest LNG facility sent [Brent crude above $119 per barrel](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/19/oil-jumps-iran-strikes-qatar-lng-facility-supply-worries.html), wiping out 17% of Qatar's gas output. The [S&P 500 dropped 0.8%, Nasdaq fell 1%](https://tradingeconomics.com/united-states/stock-market), and all three major indices hit [four-month lows](https://tradingeconomics.com/united-states/stock-market). Meanwhile, [the Federal Reserve raised its inflation forecast to 2.7%](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/federal-reserve-fomc-march-18-interest-rate-decision/) and now seven FOMC members project zero rate cuts this year—a sharp reversal.
content.analysis:
## The Headlines

When oil prices spike, stocks don't follow. That's the ugly truth playing out across markets right now.

[Brent crude briefly climbed above $119 per barrel](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/19/oil-jumps-iran-strikes-qatar-lng-facility-supply-worries.html) after Iran's missiles hit Qatar's Ras Laffan gas field—the country's largest energy site. In a single strike, [17% of Qatar's LNG output was wiped out](https://www.thestreet.com/investing/stock-market-today-mar-18-2026-stocks-futures-plummet-ahead-of-market-open-as-oil-headwinds-continue). The [S&P 500 dropped 0.8%, the Nasdaq fell 1%, and the Dow shed over 200 points](https://tradingeconomics.com/united-states/stock-market)—all [hitting four-month lows](https://tradingeconomics.com/united-states/stock-market). Chevron rallied. Everyone else? Selling.

There's a reason investors panicked. It's not just oil. It's what happens next.

## The Backstory

The Iran-Gulf conflict didn't start yesterday. [Back on February 28, 2026, the U.S. and Israel launched joint strikes on Iran](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/01/stock-market-today-live-update.html), kicking off what would become a cascade of tit-for-tat energy attacks. By early March, [the Strait of Hormuz effectively closed to most tanker traffic](https://fortune.com/2026/03/18/oil-prices-110-crude-brent-iran-war-israel-strait-hormuz-lng/) and [Brent surged past $100](https://fortune.com/2026/03/18/oil-prices-110-crude-brent-iran-war-israel-strait-hormuz-lng/).

Then came the jobs report. On March 6, [the U.S. lost 92,000 jobs](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/live/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-slide-amid-inflation-worries-as-iran-war-sends-oil-surging-133439504.html)—well below estimates of 55,000 added. That's when investors started getting nervous. [By March 13, the S&P 500 hit its year's low](https://markets.financialcontent.com/stocks/article/marketminute-2026-3-13-s-and-p-500-hits-2026-low-index-slides-15-as-staples-and-real-estate-lead-broad-sell-off).

But Thursday was worse.

## The Takes

The Federal Reserve painted a grim picture. [The Fed voted 11-1 to hold the federal funds rate at 3.5%–3.75%](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/18/fed-interest-rate-decision-march-2026.html), but here's the kicker: [seven FOMC members now project no cuts this year](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/18/fed-interest-rate-decision-march-2026.html). That's a dramatic shift. Why? [The Fed raised its inflation outlook to 2.7% for both headline and core PCE by year-end 2026](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/federal-reserve-fomc-march-18-interest-rate-decision/), up from prior estimates of 2.4% and 2.5%.

Investors had been betting on rate cuts to save the market. The Fed just took that hope away.

Meanwhile, energy markets are screaming. [Brent crude rose nearly 6% to $113.77, up from under $73 on the eve of the war](https://www.bnnbloomberg.ca/markets/oil/2026/03/19/oil-and-natural-gas-prices-soar-as-iran-attacks-gulf-energy-facilities-brent-crude-nears-us114/). [The European TTF natural gas benchmark traded 17% higher on Thursday and has doubled in the past month](https://www.bnnbloomberg.ca/markets/oil/2026/03/19/oil-and-natural-gas-prices-soar-as-iran-attacks-gulf-energy-facilities-brent-crude-nears-us114/). Analysts aren't shy about the downside either. [Citi forecasts Brent could rally to $120 near-term, with a possible $130 average in Q2–Q3 if energy infrastructure attacks continue](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/18/oil-prices-brent-wti-uae-energy-attacks-us-crude-inventories-hormuz.html).

But not everyone is panicking. [Bank of America said the energy shock is "unlikely" to trigger a recession, noting that growth expectations are improving and earnings remain positive](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/17/stock-market-today-live-updates.html). That's the bull case: this is a shock, not a collapse.

Then came Micron's earnings—which actually proves the divide. [Micron reported blowout Q2 earnings of $12.20 per share on $23.9 billion in revenue (nearly tripling year-over-year)](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/19/micron-falls-blockbuster-earnings-chips-ai.html), but [shares fell roughly 3–5% on Thursday after the company raised its capex forecast to $25 billion](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/19/micron-falls-blockbuster-earnings-chips-ai.html). Even great earnings aren't safe—not when investors are worried about oversupply and rates staying high.

## Real Talk

Here's what's actually happening: the market is getting squeezed from three directions at once.

First, there's the energy shock. [Another surge in oil spurred by the escalating Iran war sent global stocks, bonds and metals lower](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-18/stock-market-today-dow-s-p-live-updates-), with [concern intensifying that central banks will be forced to tighten policy to keep inflation in check](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-18/stock-market-today-dow-s-p-live-updates-). That's not theoretical—it's already happening in Europe. [The Bank of England held rates at 3.75% on Thursday](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/live/fed-meeting-live-updates-federal-reserve-expected-to-hold-rates-steady-offer-updated-outlook-amid-iran-war-125458502.html), and [the pan-European Stoxx 600 fell 2.76% while Germany's DAX declined 3%](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/live/fed-meeting-live-updates-federal-reserve-expected-to-hold-rates-steady-offer-updated-outlook-amid-iran-war-125458502.html).

Second, there's the bond market screaming. [Two-year Treasury yields climbed as much as 18 basis points to 3.95%, while the UK's two-year yields surged 26 basis points to 4.36%](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-18/stock-market-today-dow-s-p-live-updates-). Higher bond yields mean higher borrowing costs. That's bad for growth stocks. That's bad for anyone betting on a soft landing.

Third, there's the sector rotation trap. [Energy (+0.53%) and industrials (+0.14%) were the only S&P 500 sectors in the green on Wednesday](https://www.thestreet.com/latest-news/stock-market-today-march-18-2026). Meanwhile, [the Magnificent Seven broadly sold off—Nvidia down 1.5%, Apple and Microsoft each down more than 1%, Tesla off more than 2%](https://stockmarketwatch.com/live/stock-market-today/). The [CBOE Volatility Index (VIX) rose nearly 7% to 26.78](https://www.thestreet.com/investing/stock-market-today-mar-18-2026-stocks-futures-plummet-ahead-of-market-open-as-oil-headwinds-continue), while [gold fell 6.85% to $4,557.80 and silver plunged 12.56% as the U.S. dollar strengthened](https://www.thestreet.com/investing/stock-market-today-mar-18-2026-stocks-futures-plumment-ahead-of-market-open-as-oil-headwinds-continue).

Note what's happening: commodities are falling even as oil rises. That's a flight to safety—dollars, not growth. And on Wednesday alone, [the Dow lost 768 points (−1.63%) to 46,225, hitting a new year low](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/17/stock-market-today-live-updates.html). [The Dow is now down over 5% month-to-date, on pace for its worst month since 2022](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/17/stock-market-today-live-updates.html).

The valuation math is still okay-ish. [The S&P 500's forward P/E ratio has fallen to 20.9](https://www.schwab.com/learn/story/stock-market-update-open), slightly below the recent peak of 22 but still above the [five-year average of 20](https://www.schwab.com/learn/story/stock-market-update-open). But valuations don't matter if the world's energy infrastructure is under attack and the Fed just killed the rate-cut narrative.

## The Bottom Line

Markets are in a bind. [Another surge in oil spurred by the escalating Iran war sent global stocks, bonds and metals lower](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-18/stock-market-today-dow-s-p-live-updates-), with central banks now facing a choice between inflation and growth. The Fed just signaled it's choosing inflation—[raising its 2026 inflation projection to 2.7%](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/federal-reserve-fomc-march-18-interest-rate-decision/) and [projecting zero rate cuts from seven FOMC members](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/18/fed-interest-rate-decision-march-2026.html). That means higher rates for longer.

If you own [energy stocks like Chevron](https://www.thestreet.com/investing/stock-market-today-mar-18-2026-stocks-futures-plumment-ahead-of-market-open-as-oil-headwinds-continue), you're probably fine—or better than fine. If you own growth stocks or broad index funds, [here's what the data shows](https://tradingeconomics.com/united-states/stock-market): valuations are under pressure, volatility is rising, and the geopolitical tail risk is real. You decide what to do with that.
risks: [Citi forecasts Brent could hit $130 average in Q2–Q3 if energy infrastructure attacks continue and the Strait of Hormuz remains closed](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/18/oil-prices-brent-wti-uae-energy-attacks-us-crude-inventories-hormuz.html), which would force further central bank tightening.

[Trump threatened to 'massively blow up' the entire South Pars gas field if Iran attacked Qatar again](https://www.nbcnews.com/world/iran/live-blog/live-updates-iran-war-energy-prices-trump-gas-field-israel-qatar-rcna264010), escalating the risk of supply destruction.

[The Fed raised its inflation outlook to 2.7% for both headline and core PCE by year-end 2026](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/federal-reserve-fomc-march-18-interest-rate-decision/), reducing the likelihood of rate cuts that markets had been pricing in.

[The Dow lost 768 points and is down over 5% month-to-date, on pace for its worst month since 2022](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/17/stock-market-today-live-updates.html), signaling sustained selling pressure.
catalysts: [Bank of America said the energy shock is 'unlikely' to trigger a recession, noting that growth expectations are improving and earnings remain positive](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/17/stock-market-today-live-updates.html)—a potential floor for further declines.

[Energy (+0.53%) and industrials (+0.14%) were the only S&P 500 sectors in the green on Wednesday](https://www.thestreet.com/latest-news/stock-market-today-march-18-2026), offering relative safety plays for investors rotating out of tech.
[The S&P 500's forward P/E ratio has fallen to 20.9](https://www.schwab.com/learn/story/stock-market-update-open), providing potential value entry points if geopolitical tensions ease.

[Weekly jobless claims came in at 205,000, below expectations of 215,000](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/live/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-slide-amid-inflation-worries-as-iran-war-sends-oil-surging-133439504.html), suggesting labor markets remain resilient despite the selloff.

dataPoints.label: FOMC members projecting zero cuts in 2026

content.finalVerdict: [Brent crude briefly climbed above $119 per barrel](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/19/oil-jumps-iran-strikes-qatar-lng-facility-supply-worries.html) after Iran's strike on Qatar's largest LNG facility, [wiping out 17% of the country's gas output](https://www.thestreet.com/investing/stock-market-today-mar-18-2026-stocks-futures-plumment-ahead-of-market-open-as-oil-headwinds-continue). The [S&P 500 fell 0.8%, hitting four-month lows](https://tradingeconomics.com/united-states/stock-market), while [the Fed raised its inflation forecast to 2.7% and signaled seven FOMC members now project zero rate cuts this year](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/18/fed-interest-rate-decision-march-2026.html). If you're in broad index funds or growth stocks, the data shows rising volatility, higher bond yields, and a geopolitical tail risk that wasn't priced in. [Bank of America says recession is unlikely](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/17/stock-market-today-live-updates.html)—but higher rates for longer is almost certain. You decide what that means for your portfolio.
